title: 10 best herbal capsules for puffy eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. The aloe vera extract in these capsules reduces swelling and redness, while its moisturizing properties hydrate the skin to reduce puffiness. Additionally, aloe's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes. By taking aloe capsules regularly, individuals can enjoy a more refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce puffiness by soothing and calming irritated skin around the eyes, reducing redness and swelling.
Moisturizing: Aloe vera's rich moisturizing properties help hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, plumping up fine lines and wrinkles to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Chlorophyllin: This water-soluble derivative of chlorophyll helps reduce puffiness by drawing out excess fluids and reducing inflammation in the skin around the eyes.
Glucomannan: A type of soluble fiber found in Aloe vera, glucomannan helps to reduce puffiness by absorbing excess fluids and toxins, thus reducing swelling and inflammation.

Vitamins A and E (including beta-carotene): These antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce puffiness by protecting the skin from damage, promoting collagen production, and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel, which contains anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness.
Seeds: The seeds are used to extract aloin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes.
Buds: The buds are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of aloe vera gel from mature leaves and store it in the refrigerator for 24 hours to allow excess water to evaporate.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a food dehydrator at 55 degrees celsius for 6 hours to remove excess moisture.
Weigh 50g of dried aloe vera gel and mix it with 10g of vegetable glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the aloe vera and glycerin mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Label and store the herbal aloe vera capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and promote circulation. The capsule's active compounds, including flavonoids and antioxidants, work together to constrict blood vessels and alleviate fluid buildup, thereby reducing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, the capsule's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving them looking refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Adenosine content: Adenosine is a natural vasoconstrictor that helps to reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation, reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes.

Flavonoid compounds: Flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and edema, making them effective in alleviating puffy eyes caused by allergies or irritation.
Saponin content: Saponins are natural anti-inflammatory agents that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness by reducing water retention and improving lymphatic drainage.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can contribute to the reduction of puffiness in the eyes.
Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and alleviate the appearance of puffy eyes.
Bilobalide: This triterpenoid compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and improve the overall appearance of the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for puffy eyes because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help improve circulation and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for puffy eyes because they contain ginkgolides and bilobalide, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.

Fruits: The fruits of Ginkgo biloba are used to make capsules for puffy eyes because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help improve circulation and reduce inflammation, similar to the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves, and 200 grams of gelatin capsule powder.
Weigh out 250 milligrams of ginkgo biloba powder, and mix with 100 milligrams of gelatin.
Add 50 milligrams of silica powder to the ginkgo biloba and gelatin mixture, and stir well.
Fill the gelatin capsule powder into empty gelatin capsules, making sure not to exceed 500 milligrams per capsule.
Label and package the capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the delicate skin around the eyes. The menthol content in peppermint also helps to stimulate blood flow, reducing puffiness by draining excess fluid away from the area. Additionally, peppermint's soothing properties can help to calm irritated or red eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and revitalized. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal peppermint capsules provide a natural remedy for puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and limonene, which help reduce swelling and inflammation around the eyes, thus reducing puffiness.

Cooling and soothing effects: The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect when applied topically, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leading to reduced puffiness.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing puffiness and promoting healthy-looking skin.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and improving lymphatic drainage, which decreases swelling and reduces the appearance of puffy eyes.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and inflammation associated with puffy eyes, thereby providing relief and soothing the affected area.

Eugenol: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce puffiness by combating oxidative stress and inflammation in the eye area, thereby promoting a more comfortable and healthy appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness.
Essential oil extracted from Leaves: This essential oil is often used in eye creams and capsules to provide a cooling sensation and alleviate puffy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties.

Hydroalcoholic extract of Leaves: This is another form of concentrated peppermint extract used in capsules to help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, leading to a decrease in puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 50 grams of the powdered leaves with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 200 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams each of the leaf-gelatin mixture for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain adaptogenic compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. Anti-inflammatory activity: The herb has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and redness associated with puffy eyes, such as those caused by allergies or sinus pressure.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): As powerful antioxidants, flavonoids can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing puffiness and promoting healthy circulation around the eyes.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their content of glycosides and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce puffiness and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root during the winter months when the plant is dormant.
Dry the root in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve its potency.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 10 minutes.
Mix 5g of the powdered root with 2g of vegetable cellulose powder to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the blend in gelatin capsules with 500mg capacity using a manual capsule filler within 5 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems from a trusted source to ensure quality and potency.

Grind the dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 50 milligrams of excipients such as cellulose and magnesium stearate.
Fill the powder mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine and seal immediately.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of dried taraxacum officinale root, 250 capsules size 0, and a gelatin capsule maker.

Mix 1 gram of the powder with 250 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill the capsule maker with the gelatin mixture and fill each capsule with 250 milligrams of the mixture using the machine.
Label 250 capsules with the taraxacum officinale root powder content and store them in a cool dry place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of tanacetum parthenium flowers when in full bloom and dry them for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 1/4 cup of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of glycerin to form a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the uniform mixture using a capsule filling machine for 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which targets the underlying causes of puffiness. When taken orally, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the delicate skin around the eyes, where it reduces swelling and inflammation caused by allergens, poor circulation, or sinus pressure. As the body's natural response to these irritants subsides, the puffy appearance of the eyes begins to diminish, revealing a smoother, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by combating underlying inflammation.

Vasodilatory properties: Turmeric contains various bioactive compounds that can help dilate blood vessels, reducing water retention and alleviating fluid accumulation under the eyes, leading to a reduction in puffiness.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate puffy eyes by damaging delicate eye tissues and promoting fluid buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the turmeric plant, which stores its nutrients and contains a concentrated amount of curcuminoids that help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried turmeric root powder curcuma longa.
Mix the powder with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10ml of beeswax and stir until melted and well combined then remove from heat.
Fill empty capsules with a mixture of 400mg turmeric root powder and 20mg of vitamin k2 powder.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

Blend 15g of dried urtica dioica with 5g of carrier powder like silicon dioxide and store the mixture in an airtight container for future use.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of blended herb mixture and seal immediately to create your herbal remedy for puffy eyes preparation time approximately 30 minutes.

Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air drying for several weeks.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 30 minutes of drying completion.
Mix one part sambucus nigra powder with two parts filler such as potato starch and fill gelatin capsules with approximately 500mg of the blend within an hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to six months or until consumed within a week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain a blend of herbal extracts that work to reduce puffiness and dark circles. The antioxidants present in these capsules help to combat inflammation, while the natural astringent properties constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling. Additionally, the capsules' ability to improve circulation helps to decongest the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving you with a more refreshed and radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape capsules contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The bioactive compounds in herbal grape capsules have been shown to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and fluid accumulation under the eyes, which can lead to puffy appearance.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal grape capsules contain anti-inflammatory agents that help reduce inflammation and redness, which can exacerbate puffiness and make it appear more pronounced.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, grape roots help reduce puffiness by soothing irritated skin and reducing fluid retention.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, grape seeds have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and promote healthy circulation around the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100g of dried vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in a clean glass container.
Combine measured herb with 200ml of boiling water and let steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container.
Mix 10g of glycerin and 50g of water to create a filling solution and combine it with the herbal extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal-filling solution and seal them tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain potent botanicals that work to reduce inflammation and swelling. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in herbal teas such as green tea, chamomile, and peppermint help to constrict blood vessels and relax facial muscles, reducing the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, these herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, leaving them looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Herbs like Eyebright, Chamomile, and Green Tea contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the eye area.

Antioxidant Activity: Antioxidants present in herbal tea capsules like Turmeric, Ginger, and Peppermint help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to puffy eyes, promoting a smoother and more radiant complexion.

Vasoconstrictive Effects: Herbs like Bilberry, Gotu Kola, and Horse Chestnut contain vasoconstrictive compounds that help reduce blood flow to the affected area, reducing puffiness and dark circles by tightening up dilated blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, tea roots help reduce swelling and discoloration caused by puffy eyes.
Buds: The young buds of the tea plant contain flavonoids and caffeine, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness.
Bark: Tea bark has been traditionally used to treat eye issues due to its antibacterial properties, which may help soothe and protect irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure two tablespoons of dried camellia sinensis leaves and one tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Grind the ingredients into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered blend with 250 milliliters of boiling water for three minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to extract the tea liquid for fifteen minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the cooled tea liquid and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with puffy eyes because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and relax facial muscles. The herbal extract contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that combats free radicals and minimizes puffiness. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties help to soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and swelling. As a result, these capsules can provide effective relief from puffy eyes, leaving you with a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness around the eyes, alleviating puffiness.

Calming and relaxing properties: Lemon balm has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax facial muscles and reduce tension that may contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and soothing irritated skin.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, alleviating puffy eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves from a reputable source.
Grind the leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a glass bowl.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 24 hours to allow for extraction of active compounds.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the liquid extract and seal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with puffy eyes because they promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, which are two major contributors to puffiness. The capsules contain valerenic acid, a compound that has a sedative effect on the body, calming the nervous system and reducing stress levels. This, in turn, helps to decrease the appearance of dark circles and swelling under the eyes. Additionally, valerian's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and puffiness, leaving the area looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian root has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of puffiness around the eyes, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye area, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Valerian has been shown to constrict blood vessels, which can help reduce fluid buildup and puffiness around the eyes by reducing blood flow to the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem of the plant, rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative effect on the body, helping to alleviate eye strain and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 cups of dried valerian root and transfer it to a clean glass container for storage.
Grind the dried valerian root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground valerian root with 50 grams of organic tapioca starch in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Add 100 ml of cold-pressed coconut oil to the mixture and stir until well combined for 3 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal blend mixture and store them in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for puffy eyes are natural supplements designed to alleviate swelling and discoloration around the eyes, providing relief and a more refreshed appearance. The best herbal capsules for puffy eyes include Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Euphrasia officinalis, Vitis vinifera, Ruscus aculeatus, Silybum marianum, Centella asiatica, Cucumis sativus, Echinacea purpurea, and Camellia sinensis. If left untreated, puffy eyes can negatively impact daily life, causing fatigue, dark circles, and a diminished sense of well-being, ultimately affecting one's ability to work and engage in social activities with confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal capsules are dark circles, under-eye bags, and sinusitis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like bilberry, grape seed extract, and horse chestnut can help reduce puffiness by improving blood circulation, soothing inflammation, and strengthening capillaries. Other benefits include reducing fluid retention, alleviating sinus pressure, and promoting overall eye health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with puffy eyes is Caffeine and Peppermint, combined with the anti-inflammatory properties of Ginger and Bromelain. Caffeine reduces water retention, while Peppermint cools and soothes the skin. Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness, and Bromelain, an enzyme found in pineapple, breaks down proteins that cause swelling. Taking these herbal capsules together can provide a powerful combination to alleviate puffy eyes and leave you looking refreshed and rejuvenated. This blend can be taken as directed, usually 1-2 capsules daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain the soothing and anti-inflammatory properties of pure aloe vera. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has been shown to reduce swelling and redness, while also hydrating the delicate skin around the eyes. By reducing puffiness and dark circles, aloe vera capsules can give the appearance of brighter, more refreshed eyes. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for those seeking a non-invasive way to combat puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with puffy eyes, making it easier to relax and reduce eye puffiness.
Catechin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help decrease puffiness and redness in the eyes, promoting a more relaxed and refreshed appearance.
Salicylic acid: Aids in reducing inflammation and swelling, while also promoting blood flow and draining excess fluids, which contributes to the reduction of puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.

Gel from the leaves: The gel is used because it contains a mixture of water and aloe vera's beneficial compounds, which help to reduce inflammation, hydrate the skin, and reduce the appearance of puffy eyes.

Extract from the leaves: The extract is used because it contains a concentrated form of aloe vera's active ingredients, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 aloe barbadensis leaves when mature and allow them to dry in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks.
Peel the dried leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix the powder with 500mg of silica gel as a filler to enhance capsule stability and flow properties.
Use a capsule machine to fill 500mg capsules with the powder mixture at a rate of 50 capsules per minute.
Store the herbal aloe barbadensis capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with puffy eyes because of their potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work by improving blood circulation and reducing swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes. Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenoids help to relax blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup, leading to a decrease in puffiness and a more refreshed appearance. As a result, ginkgo biloba capsules can help to alleviate puffy eyes and leave the skin looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce puffiness by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a triterpene in Ginkgo biloba, helps reduce puffiness by promoting lymphatic drainage and reducing fluid accumulation in the tissues around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation.
Seeds: Contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce puffiness.
Buds: Contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve lymphatic drainage, reducing puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 gram of ginkgo biloba extract powder per capsule.
Weigh and mix 5 grams of gelatin and 1 gram of glycerin in a bowl.
Heat the gelatin mixture in a double boiler at 160-170 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Add the ginkgo biloba extract powder to the gelatin mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden before filling capsules with 0.5 gram each.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These antioxidants work to reduce inflammation and swelling, which are common causes of puffy eyes. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis capsules have anti-edematous properties, helping to reduce water retention and alleviate the puffiness. By targeting the underlying causes of puffiness, Euphrasia officinalis capsules provide a natural and effective solution for achieving a more refreshed and radiant appearance.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can contribute to the reduction of puffiness in the eyes.
Phenolic acids: They possess antioxidant properties that help to prevent oxidative stress and inflammation, which can cause puffiness in the eyes.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to reduce swelling and improve the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes.
Flowers: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, helping to alleviate puffy eyes and dark circles.
Roots: They are used due to their potential to reduce swelling and inflammation, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 50 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of powdered euphrasia officinalis with 250 milligrams of vegetable cellulose in a small bowl.
Fill 100 vegetable capsules with the euphrasia officinalis mixture using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and promote relaxation. The botanical properties of these capsules work to alleviate puffiness by improving blood circulation and reducing fluid retention. This, in turn, helps to diminish the appearance of dark circles and bags under the eyes. Additionally, Vitis vinifera capsules promote a healthy and balanced skin environment, leading to smoother, more radiant-looking skin and reduced puffiness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate puffy eyes.
Tannins: As astringent agents, tannins help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, thereby minimizing puffiness around the eyes.
Polyphenols: With their antioxidant properties, polyphenols may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to a reduction in puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Fruits: They are a good source of vitamin C and flavonoids, which help to tighten the skin, reduce swelling, and improve circulation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of dried vitis vinifera roots with 70% ethanol at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the extract through a cheesecloth into a glass container to separate the liquid from solids.
Evaporate the liquid extract using a rotary evaporator at 40 degrees celsius to concentrate the extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the concentrated extract powder.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place to preserve their potency for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and promote relaxation. The extract from the Ruscus plant has been used in traditional medicine to combat water retention and promote lymphatic drainage. By improving circulation and reducing fluid buildup, Ruscus aculeatus capsules can help to alleviate puffy eyes, leaving you with a brighter and more refreshed appearance. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief without the need for harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help with puffy eyes by reducing inflammation and improving lymphatic drainage, which in turn reduces swelling.
Triterpenoids: They possess anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate puffiness by soothing and calming irritated tissues around the eyes.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to reduce fluid retention and inflammation, thereby minimizing the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Roots: Roots are used due to their ability to calm and reduce inflammation, making them suitable for addressing puffy eyes.
Barks: Barks are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help tighten and soothe the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ruscus aculeatus leaves in a dehydrator at 150°f for 2 hours or in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1000mg of the ruscus aculeatus powder with 500mg of tapioca starch and 250mg of gelatin to create a filling mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the ruscus aculeatus mixture using a capsule filler and 500mg per capsule.
Store the herbal ruscus aculeatus capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years to maintain their potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain Silymarin, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and improves lymphatic drainage. The Silymarin in these capsules has anti-edema properties that help to reduce water retention and fluid buildup under the eyes, thus reducing puffiness. Additionally, Silymarin's ability to improve microcirculation and enhance the body's natural detoxification processes helps to flush out toxins and excess fluids that can cause puffy eyes. This results in a more refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: It helps with puffy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate the discomfort associated with puffiness.
Flavonoids: They help with puffy eyes by acting as powerful antioxidants, which protect against oxidative stress and promote vascular health, reducing the appearance of puffiness.
Polyphenols: These compounds help with puffy eyes by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that contributes to inflammation and swelling, thereby reducing the severity of puffiness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate puffy eyes.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat eye problems, including puffiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and seeds from mature plants at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the silybum marianum flowers and seeds in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried silybum marianum flowers and seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica capsules helps with puffy eyes because it promotes collagen production and reduces inflammation, resulting in improved skin elasticity and firmness. The antioxidants present in Centella asiatica, also known as Gotu Kola, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause puffiness and swelling. By reducing water retention and improving circulation, Centella asiatica capsules can help to depuff the eyes, leaving them looking smoother and more refreshed. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief from puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Tricin: Helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, contributing to the alleviation of puffy eyes.
Asiatic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in minimizing puffiness and promoting healthy blood flow in the eye area.
Madecassoside: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to calm and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes and reduce swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness.
Roots: The roots are often used due to their rich content of asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used due to their content of triterpenoid saponins, which are believed to help reduce puffiness and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems from a clean environment to ensure quality.
Dry the harvested centella asiatica in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Grind 5 grams of dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground centella asiatica powder with 250 milligrams of gelatin or vegetable cellulose.
Fill 100-count capsules with the powder mixture using an automatic capsule filler and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cucumis sativus capsules helps with puffy eyes because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, commonly known as cucumber, is known to reduce water retention and swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes. By improving blood circulation and reducing puffiness, Cucumis sativus capsules help to leave the eyes looking refreshed and revitalized. Additionally, the cooling and soothing properties of the herb can calm irritated skin and provide a sense of relaxation and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Fisetin: This flavonoid constituent helps reduce puffiness by inhibiting the production of vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) and reducing inflammation.
Cucurbitacins: These triterpenoid saponins help reduce puffiness by inhibiting the activity of inflammatory enzymes and reducing the permeability of blood vessels.
Silica: This inorganic compound helps reduce puffiness by improving lymphatic drainage and reducing the retention of fluid in the tissues.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, alleviating puffy eyes.
Fruits (or Cucumber): They are used due to their high water content and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce puffiness and hydrate the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cucumis sativus fruits at peak ripeness when they are fully yellow and the skin is tender to the touch.
Dry cucumis sativus fruits in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 24 to 48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind dried cucumis sativus fruits into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 5 grams per serving.
Mix the powder with a small amount of tapioca starch or gelatin to facilitate capsule formation at a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powder mixture at a dosage of 5 grams per capsule and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with puffy eyes because of their anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that help reduce swelling and alleviate congestion. By soothing the blood vessels and promoting lymphatic drainage, Echinacea purpurea capsules can reduce puffiness and promote a smoother, more refreshed appearance. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can contribute to eye puffiness, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for combating puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These constituents help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, contributing to the alleviation of puffy eyes.
Iridoid glycosides: They exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help minimize the puffiness associated with eye inflammation.
Phenolic acids: These constituents possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and redness in the eyes, thus reducing puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of the active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help reduce puffiness and inflammation.

Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain a variety of compounds, including alkylamides and caftaric acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 echinacea purpurea roots and clean them under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 35-40°c for 2-3 hours using a food dehydrator.
Grind 2-3 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500 mg of gelatin and add 10-20 ml of water to create a gel.
Fill gelatin capsules with the echinacea purpurea gel and store them in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis capsules helps with puffy eyes because they contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and combat free radicals that can cause puffiness. Additionally, the theaflavins present in Camellia sinensis capsules have been shown to improve circulation, allowing excess fluids to be drained away from the eyes. This results in a reduction of puffiness and a more refreshed appearance. Regular use of these capsules may lead to a long-term reduction in puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Help reduce puffiness by acting as a vasoconstrictor, narrowing blood vessels and decreasing fluid accumulation around the eyes.
Quinic acid derivatives: Assist in reducing inflammation and fluid retention, contributing to the alleviation of puffy eyes.
Theaflavins: Possess antioxidant properties that may help reduce swelling and inflammation around the eyes, contributing to a decrease in puffiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make eye care capsules due to their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Buds: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and calm irritated skin around the eyes.
Seeds: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh camellia sinensis leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 30g of the powdered leaves with 30g of a binding agent and 10g of a filler in a bowl.
Fill 200-250 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them securely with a capsule filler.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
